alert: orders-api p99 latency went from 200ms to 25s over 15 minutes. About a third of POST /orders are timing out at the gateway. Host CPU and memory flat. Database CPU normal. We shipped a retry wrapper nine days ago.
why: Different service, same signature: latency climbing while CPU stays flat, after a retry change. The agent must generalize across services.

alert: Catalog service dashboard is entirely green but merchants say new products take hours to appear in the storefront. Consumer lag is zero.
why: Green dashboard contradicting user-visible behavior -- a silent drop path, not a lag problem.

alert: Two customers report being billed twice for the same subscription renewal. Worker logs show the same renewal id handled by two pods within about 300ms.
why: Read-then-write idempotency race. Must recall the unique-constraint fix, not suggest more caching.

alert: Finance says the monthly rollup is about 10% under what the source system reports. The nightly job exits zero every single night.
why: Silent row loss with a success exit code.

alert: After we failed over to the secondary region, session validation in APAC went from 80ms to 1.6s. Error rate is still exactly zero. Traffic is unchanged.
why: Zero errors plus multiplied latency after failover means cross-region routing, not database slowness.

alert: Users can attach small PDFs fine, but anything over ~10MB comes back 502. Nothing at all in the application logs for the failures.
why: No app log line means the failure is upstream of the app -- an LB body limit.

alert: Landing page time-to-first-byte doubled right after the release. CPU is flat. Our config vendor is reporting degraded performance in one region.
why: Blocking third-party call in the request path with no timeout.

alert: The weekly report job ran twice last Sunday, four minutes apart, after we drained a node for patching. Some accounts got duplicate emails.
why: Schedulers are at-least-once; job needs its own lock.

alert: The office espresso machine is making a grinding noise and the milk frother stopped working.
why: Control case. Memory contains nothing relevant. The agent must return no match rather than stretch one.

alert: Kubernetes ingress controller pods are in CrashLoopBackOff after a cluster upgrade, with TLS handshake errors in the logs.
why: Plausible-sounding infra alert with no true precedent in memory. Tests that we do not manufacture a false match.
